target.name: Applied Atomics
target.founder: Ben Kellie, Paul Keutelian, Mark Lambert

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Applied Atomics is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Ben Kellie, Paul Keutelian, and Mark Lambert. The company has raised $10.3M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Exodys Energy.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy cohort—only 0 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met filters, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum for reliable cross-sectional analysis. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions to the $10.3M capital raised proxy, yielding a point estimate of $25.75M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $22M to $30M, centered on $26M. This range reflects typical early-stage expansion dynamics but carries material uncertainty due to reliance on a single methodological leg. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 2.5x multiplier to frontier-tech nuclear subsectors, the company's progress against development milestones, and relative valuation momentum within the broader nuclear peer set. Additional validation would require either sufficient same-stage nuclear comps or direct market signals from ongoing fundraising.
